titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Toi Derricotte papers
titlestmt.author: by Jessica Tai

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday July 23, 2019 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: This collection contains writings, correspondence, journals, photographs, personal papers, teaching material, electronic media, audio recordings, videocassettes, and other material by or relating to poet, writer, and educator, Toi Derricotte. Material related to Cave Canem, the non-profit organization committed to cultivating the artistic and professional growth of African American poets, is also represented.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe Rare Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Writings, circa 1960s-2010s. II. Correspondence, circa 1960s-2010s. III. Professional papers, 1979-2015. IV. Personal papers, 1934-circa 2010s. V. Audiovisual material, 1967-2006. VI. Electronic media, circa 1990s-2000s. VII. Restricted papers, circa 1980s-2000s.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Toi Derricotte Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Toi Derricotte Papers. James Weldon Johnson Collection in the Yale Collection of American Literature. Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

Information included in the Content Description note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
Folder titles enclosed in quotation marks indicate transcribed writing from original enclosures.

bioghist.head: Toi Derricotte
bioghist.p: Toi Derricotte is an American poet, writer, educator, and co-founder of the Cave Canem Foundation. She was born in 1941 in Hamtramck, Michigan and received her B.A. from Wayne State University and an M.A. in English Literature and Creative Writing from New York University. Her published works include, Natural Birth (1983), Captivity (1989), Tender (1997), The Black Notebooks (1997), and The Undertaker's Daughter (2011). In 1996, together with Cornelius Eady, Derricotte cofounded the Cave Canem Foundation, a national poetry organization committed to cultivating the artistic and professional growth of African American poets.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Cave Canem records (JWJ MSS 130). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 107, 108, 109, and 110 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 111 and 112 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 113: (restricted): For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains writings, poems and notebooks by Derricotte, in addition to works by others. Some materials contain corrections by Derricotte.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is primarily arranged in its received order. Notebooks have been arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Typed and handwritten poems and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Works include: "The Picture of Gertrude Stein," "Wedding Poem," and "The Education of the Royal Bride."

scopecontent.p: Works include: "The Head of Hollefernes," "The Red Teapot," and "Natural Birth."

scopecontent.p: Works include: "The Real Tree," "Connections to a Son Who Has Left Home," and "She/I."

processinfo.p: Inactive mold present. Use caution when handling.

scopecontent.p: Includes works by Afaa M. Weaver, Madeline Tiger, Sharon Olds, and William Packard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains personal correspondence and greeting cards from Derricotte's friends and family. Some professional material, such as contracts for Derricotte's speaking engagements, is also included.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged in its received order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Audre Lorde, Sharon Old, and Joanne Braxton. Also includes literary works sent by correspondents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Cornelius Eady and John Brian Weller. Also includes photographs and negatives with images of Derricotte.

did.unittitle: Personal and professional correspondence, printed e-mail correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Joanne Braxton, Fiona Cheong, Michael Harper, Terrance Hayes, Maxine Kumin, Jonathan Galassi, and Michael Weaver. Includes material relating to Cave Canem and a chapbook by Jessica Fjeld.

did.unittitle: Personal and professional correspondence, and professional material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Fiona Cheong and Aundra Willis. Also includes photographs with images of Derricotte and material relating to Derricotte's speaking engagements.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Dinah Berland, Rita Dove, Audre Lorde, Don Mattera, and Suzanne Rhodenbaugh.

did.unittitle: Personal and professional correspondence, and printed e-mail correspondence

scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Fiona Cheong, Sharon Olds, Adrienne Rich, and John Brian Weller. Also includes photographs with images of Derricotte.

scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with John Brian Weller.

scopecontent.p: Includes contracts for Derricotte's speaking engagements.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with John Brian Weller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains professional correspondence, speaking engagement material, and teaching material, including syllabi and letters of support. Also included is material relating to Derricotte's published works.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged in its received order.

did.unittitle: Tenure material, professional contracts, and writing engagement and conference ephemera

processinfo.p: Inactive mold present. Use caution when handling.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes material related to "Natural Birth," "The Black Notebooks," "Captivity," and "Creative Writing: A Manual for Teachers."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographs, photo albums, scrapbooks, yearbooks, juvenalia, and other personal material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged in its received order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs with inscriptions by John Brian Weller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes early family photographs, baby books, and scrapbooks containing correspondence and ephemera, compiled by Derricotte and her family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes early family photographs, correspondence, clippings, and publications.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Derricotte and her immediate family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Derricotte's Mother, Antonia Cryrus' wedding.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Email correspondence from Toi Derricotte to Nancy Kuhl on 3-21-19: "[The 1926 Webster's dictionary played] an important part in my writing because that was the dictionary that belonged to my father, given to him by my grandmother when he graduated from embalming school. I refer to it in The Undertakers Daughter, how my father used the illustrations of muscles to teach me the body."

did.unittitle: Family and genealogical material, yearbook, diploma, and address book
Notes, financial planning documents, house listings, and business cards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains audiovisual material including audiocassettes, microcassettes, VHS tapes and U-matic videocassettes.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Randall Jarrell Reading at Wayne State

Jean Binta Breeze Riding On De Riddym
Remember to Trip the Clown / Wee C Squared
Everything Rhymes With You / Deep Wee
Benjamin Zephaniah - "Overstanding" / Hughes, Frost, Williams, Wilbur
Music Mix - To Toi With Love
#2 Century of Recorded Voices, Kerouac / Walcott / Snyder / Wakokski (empty cassette case)
Toi Derricotte MHS Alver Brown's Class
"A Poet's Voice" Poetry by Naomi Long Madgett
"Consciousness and Race: Interior Journeys Towards Identity" No Limits Conference, Lincoln, Nebraska
Toi Derricotte Writers at Rutgers Series
The Usual Suspects Documentary on Audre Lorde

scopecontent.p: This series contains electronic media including CD-Rs and floppy disks.
